title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
summary: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) is an open source framework for building long-running, stateless, event-driven AI systems using a coordinator agent pattern with multi-agent delegation. The tutorial demonstrates how sub-agents handle specialized tasks while maintaining clean reasoning chains.
writeup: The video covers building long-running AI agents with Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK), an open source framework for stateless, event-driven systems organized around a coordinator agent pattern with multi-agent delegation. It shows how sub-agents take on specialized tasks while the system keeps reasoning chains clean. The takeaways center on the architecture: a coordinator that delegates to sub-agents, and an event-driven, stateless design suited to long-running workloads.
